retrieval_text: Blue Cohosh is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 28 works and 69 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Blue Cohosh. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Bark, Berry, Flower, Leaf, Tops. Common preparation co-mentions include Tincture, Tea, Powder, Extract, Decoction, Bath. Common condition co-mentions include Fever, Cold or catarrh, Colic, Epilepsy or convulsions, Rheumatism, Cholera. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Child-use caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Pregnancy caution, Abortifacient warning, Allergy or sensitivity caution. Representative source works include The antiseptic or commonsense practice of medicine and guide to health : for the use of all true friends of medical science / by R.J. Parsons.; The American family physician, or, Domestic guide to health : prepared expressly for the use of families, in language adapted to the understanding of the people : arranged in two divisions; The American eclectic dispensatory; The dispensatory and pharmacopoeia of North America and Great Britain / by John Buchanan and John J. Siggins.. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
